header.label: Student Migration Analytics
header.headline: 2.6 Cr student migration records — where is movement concentrated?
header.sub: Migration records track movement across migration type, school location, management category, and selected student markers such as BPL and CWSN status.

cards.label: Total Migrants
cards.headline: 2.6 Cr
cards.note: Total migration records stand at 2.6 Cr. Intra-District movement accounts for 87.1%, Inter-District for 11.9%, and Inter-State for 1.0%.

cards.label: Migration Type
cards.note: Intra-District migration is the dominant movement stream at 87.1%. Inter-District accounts for 11.9%, while Inter-State remains limited at 1.0%.

cards.label: CWSN Profile
cards.headlineNote: Migrated CWSN students as share of all migrants
cards.note: Migrated CWSN students account for 1.1% of all migration records. The origin-side CWSN flag is 1.0% and the destination-side CWSN flag is 0.9%.

cards.label: BPL Profile
cards.headlineNote: Current BPL share among migrant records
cards.note: Current BPL status is reported for 41.4% of migrant records, compared with 41.3% under previous BPL status.

2022-23.locationSource: Rural-to-Rural movement remains the principal corridor at 55.9%, followed by Urban-to-Urban movement at 25.5%.
2022-23.locationNote: Cross-location movement accounts for 18.6% in aggregate, comprising Rural→Urban (12.7%) and Urban→Rural (5.9%).
2022-23.mgmtSource: State Govt → State Govt is the largest management corridor at 35.3%, followed by Private Unaided → Private Unaided at 21.6%.
2022-23.mgmtNote: Management corridors are shown as a share of all migration records for the year.

insight1.label: Current BPL Share in Migration Records
insight1.note: 1.1 Cr migrant records carry current BPL status, equivalent to 41.4% of the yearly total.

insight2.label: Inter-State Migration Share
insight2.note: Inter-State movement accounts for 1.0% of migration records, representing 2.7 L students.

2023-24.locationSource: Rural-to-Rural movement remains the principal corridor at 56.7%, followed by Urban-to-Urban movement at 24.8%.
